 {"id":"bd-2ao3","title":"[10.21] Implement temporal drift feature engine (velocity, acceleration, entropy, novelty, capability-creep gradient).","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.21 — Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)

Why This Exists:
BPET longitudinal phenotype intelligence track for pre-compromise trajectory detection and integration with DGIS/ATC/migration/economic policy.

Task Objective:
Implement temporal drift feature engine (velocity, acceleration, entropy, novelty, capability-creep gradient).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Drift features are numerically stable and reproducible; feature-store interfaces support historical replay and windowed recomputation.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/security/bpet/drift_features.rs`, `tests/security/bpet_drift_feature_stability.rs`, `artifacts/10.21/bpet_drift_feature_matrix.csv`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-2ao3/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-2ao3/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.21] Implement temporal drift feature engine (velocity, acceleration, entropy, novelty, capability-creep gradient).", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.21] Implement temporal drift feature engine (velocity, acceleration, entropy, novelty, capability-creep gradient)." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.21] Implement temporal drift feature engine (velocity, acceleration, entropy, novelty, capability-creep gradient)." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.21] Implement temporal drift feature engine (velocity, acceleration, entropy, novelty, capability-creep gradient)." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.21] Implement temporal drift feature engine (velocity, acceleration, entropy, novelty, capability-creep gradient)." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Drift features are numerically stable and reproducible; feature-store interfaces support historical replay and windowed recomputation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:08.033916716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:09:03.957096978Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:09:03.957068605Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-21","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ao3","depends_on_id":"bd-2xgs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:05:31.045200172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2avg","title":"NaN/Inf guards: vef_perf_budget_gate noise_tolerance + regression_threshold, object_class_tuning overhead_ratio","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-07T11:36:40.774009382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T11:36:44.437495090Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T11:36:44.437399943Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2avo","title":"[10.3 bug] Replace placeholder migration autopilot and signed artifact shortcuts with evidence-backed analyzer/planner/validator","description":"Why this exists:
The current product-layer migration autopilot in `crates/franken-node/src/connector/migration_pipeline.rs` still uses placeholder decision logic (`blocked_` / `fail_verify_` prefixes, fabricated plan hashes, decorative `sig_` receipt strings), and adjacent migration artifact surfaces still lean on synthetic signatures. That leaves the non-engine replacement story materially incomplete even if franken_engine catches up.

What this bead must do:
Replace placeholder migration analysis/planning/verification and synthetic artifact signing with a real evidence-backed pipeline that fuses project scanner output, compatibility oracle evidence, dependency topology, measured validation outcomes, and canonical signed migration artifacts. The design should lean into frontier tactics that actually change the curve: deterministic abstract interpretation over JS/TS project surfaces, VOI-budgeted lockstep scheduling, calibrated uncertainty intervals, minimal counterexample extraction, and rollout synthesis that optimizes safe throughput rather than toy pass/fail heuristics.

Acceptance criteria:
1. `run_analysis` derives risk findings from real project facts (API/runtime/dependency inventory, compatibility bands, known divergences, corpus coverage, extension capability needs) and must not classify success/failure from name prefixes or synthetic string tests.
2. `generate_plan` emits a deterministic `shadow -> canary -> ramp -> default` rollout plan whose phase choices are justified by measured risk, dependency constraints, and rollback cost; each phase carries a rollback certificate and explanation trace.
3. `run_verification` consumes real lockstep/project evidence and returns calibrated uncertainty artifacts (Wilson/Jeffreys/conformal-style intervals or equivalent deterministic calibration) instead of unconditional placeholder passes.
4. Migration receipts and migration artifacts are canonically serialized and cryptographically authenticated; decorative `sig_` strings or fabricated signature prefixes do not satisfy the replacement-critical path.
5. Failing migrations produce a minimal counterexample witness bundle that identifies the smallest observed incompatibility slice needed to reproduce the failure.
6. Degraded mode is explicit: when an evidence source is unavailable, the pipeline widens uncertainty / lowers confidence and emits a structured degraded-mode report; it must never silently fabricate a green result.
7. Add verification that no replacement-critical migration path still branches on `blocked_`, `fail_verify_`, fabricated plan hashes, or synthetic signature shortcuts.
8. Deliver unit tests, integration/E2E fixtures, structured logs, and machine-readable evidence artifacts proving determinism, calibration behavior, signed-artifact integrity, and rollback-plan soundness.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-08T23:14:41.995760099Z","created_by":"RoseMountain","updated_at":"2026-03-09T00:45:53.704825818Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T00:45:53.704804258Z","close_reason":"Implemented evidence-backed migration pipeline/analyzer/planner/validator and authenticated migration artifacts; validated with rch check/clippy + targeted tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-optimization","extreme-optimization","gap-closure","replacement-critical","section-10-3"]}

 {"id":"bd-2bj4","title":"[10.20] Implement deterministic graph ingestion pipeline from lockfiles, extension manifests, registry metadata, and local execution evidence.","description":"## Why This Exists

The DGIS graph schema (bd-b541) defines the data model, but a schema without a reliable ingestion pipeline is an empty container. This bead builds the deterministic pipeline that populates the DGIS graph from real-world evidence: lockfiles (Cargo.lock, package-lock.json, etc.), extension manifests, registry metadata, and local execution evidence collected by the franken_node runtime.

Determinism is non-negotiable: given the same source set, the pipeline must produce the identical graph state every time, enabling replay-based debugging, audit trails, and CI reproducibility. When provenance data is stale, missing, or ambiguous, the pipeline must surface this as typed risk signals rather than silently inferring or dropping edges. This is the foundational data-quality gate that every downstream DGIS computation (topology metrics, fragility models, contagion simulation) depends on.

Within the 9N enhancement map, this bead is the second link in the critical chain: bd-b541 (schema) -> bd-2bj4 (ingestion) -> bd-t89w (metrics) / bd-2jns (fragility) / bd-cclm (adversarial validation).

## What This Must Do

1. Implement a deterministic graph ingestion pipeline that accepts lockfiles, extension manifests, registry metadata, and local execution evidence as inputs.
2. Resolve graph state reproducibly from the same source set -- byte-identical graph serialization on repeated ingestion runs.
3. Classify every discovered dependency edge using the canonical edge-type taxonomy from the graph schema (bd-b541): runtime, build, provenance, optional, dev-only.
4. Surface stale or missing provenance as typed risk signals (not silent omissions) with structured risk-signal codes.
5. Support ingestion replay: given a captured source-set snapshot, re-ingestion produces the identical graph.
6. Handle conflicting evidence gracefully: when lockfile and registry metadata disagree, emit a typed conflict signal and apply a documented resolution policy.
7. Emit structured ingestion telemetry: per-source parse counts, edge-type distribution, risk-signal counts, ingestion duration, and determinism-verification hashes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Ingestion reproducibly resolves graph state from the same source set; stale/missing provenance is explicitly surfaced as typed risk signals; ingestion supports replay.
- Given a captured source-set fixture, two independent ingestion runs produce byte-identical graph serializations.
- At least 5 distinct source types are supported (Cargo.lock, package-lock.json, extension manifest, registry JSON, execution evidence log).
- Every risk signal has a stable typed code and structured payload.
- Replay mode accepts a source-set snapshot directory and produces a graph + ingestion report without network access.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: parser coverage for each source type (lockfile, manifest, registry, execution evidence); edge-type classification correctness; risk-signal emission for missing/stale provenance; conflict resolution policy behavior.
- Integration tests: full pipeline round-trip from fixture source-set to serialized graph with determinism verification; replay mode end-to-end; multi-source-type ingestion with cross-source conflict scenarios.
- Conformance tests at `tests/conformance/dgis_graph_ingestion.rs`: validate that ingestion output matches expected golden graphs for canonical source-set fixtures.
- Structured logging: ingestion events with stable codes (DGIS-INGEST-001 through DGIS-INGEST-NNN); per-source parse telemetry; trace correlation IDs; risk-signal emission log.
- Deterministic replay: source-set fixtures checked into repository enable CI replay without network dependencies.

## Expected Artifacts

- `src/security/dgis/graph_ingestion.rs` -- ingestion pipeline implementation
- `tests/conformance/dgis_graph_ingestion.rs` -- conformance test suite
- `artifacts/10.20/dgis_ingestion_replay_report.json` -- replay verification report
- `artifacts/section_10_20/bd-2bj4/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_20/bd-2bj4/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies

- bd-b541 (blocks) -- [10.20] Define canonical dependency/topology graph schema: provides the schema that this pipeline populates","acceptance_criteria":"- Ingestion reproducibly resolves graph state from the same source set; stale/missing provenance is explicitly surfaced as typed risk signals; ingestion supports replay.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:06.502245942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:08:21.040852067Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:08:21.040823304Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-20","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bj4","depends_on_id":"bd-b541","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T17:04:49.192534671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
